name: Little Rock AFB
submarket: Jacksonville / Cabot / Sherwood / North Little Rock
profile: Military-friendly Little Rock AFB housing snapshot anchored to Jacksonville, Cabot, Sherwood, North Little Rock, Maumelle, Beebe, and the Central Arkansas metro, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Affordable Central Arkansas military airlift market with low commute pressure, strong payment accessibility, and neighborhood-by-neighborhood condition variation

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on April 2026 Central Arkansas VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Little Rock AFB buyers should model mortgage, property tax, insurance, utilities, commute, property condition, roof/HVAC age, and assignment length together.
Jacksonville and Cabot can offer strong affordability and practical commute access, but exact neighborhood, school zone, and property condition still matter.
Lower purchase prices can make ownership attractive, but older-home condition, tornado/severe-weather exposure, drainage, and resale logic should be reviewed before committing.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan estimate for Jacksonville / Little Rock AFB area

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military access with controlled entry and 24/7 base security.
crime_status.metro_jacksonville_central_arkansas: Jacksonville, Cabot, Sherwood, North Little Rock, and surrounding Central Arkansas areas vary by exact neighborhood, school zone, property condition, and commute route. Military families should evaluate exact address, schools, safety, drainage, roof/HVAC condition, and resale logic before committing.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 school system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Jacksonville North Pulaski School District, Cabot Public Schools, Pulaski County Special School District, Sherwood, North Little Rock, Beebe, and surrounding districts vary by exact address. Families should verify school assignment, commute, and support services before choosing housing.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by Little Rock AFB, C-130 airlift mission demand, training and permanent-party turnover, Jacksonville-area supply, and broader Central Arkansas workforce demand.

rental_metrics.notes: Zillow-style rental observations show Jacksonville average rent around $1,150-$1,200, with 3-bedroom rental planning estimates near $1,295 depending on condition, location, and property type.

special_events: Riverfest-style Little Rock regional events when scheduled

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Major C-130 airlift and training base with affordable housing, low commute pressure, strong Central Arkansas military identity, and practical family housing options across Jacksonville, Cabot, Sherwood, and North Little Rock.

financial_brief.bah_vs_buy_position: Strong with condition and timeline discipline
financial_brief.affordability_summary: Little Rock AFB offers one of the more accessible Air Force purchase-price environments, but buyers should still account for property condition, roof/HVAC age, tornado/severe-weather exposure, drainage, and whether the assignment timeline supports ownership.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers targeting clean, well-located homes in Jacksonville, Cabot, Sherwood, North Little Rock, or Beebe with practical commute, strong condition, and clear resale logic.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Little Rock AFB gives military families strong affordability and manageable commute options, but the best housing move depends on property condition, school zone, commute route, resale logic, and assignment length.

summary_points: Little Rock AFB sits near Jacksonville, Arkansas and serves as a major C-130 airlift and training hub.
Jacksonville offers the closest practical housing lane, while Cabot, Sherwood, North Little Rock, Maumelle, Beebe, and Ward provide different school, commute, and lifestyle tradeoffs.
Purchase prices are highly accessible compared with many military markets, but property condition and neighborhood selection still matter.
The best homes are not simply the cheapest homes; they are clean, insurable, well-maintained, and practical for resale or rental exit.
The best Little Rock AFB housing decision balances BAH, commute, schools, condition, taxes, utilities, and assignment length.
opportunities: Lower purchase prices than most Air Force markets.
Short commute options in Jacksonville can simplify daily life.
Cabot and Sherwood may offer stronger family lifestyle appeal for households accepting a longer commute.
Military-connected demand supports rental relevance near Jacksonville and base-adjacent corridors.
VA buyers can often find practical ownership paths when property condition and residual income are controlled.
risks: Older housing stock may require roof, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, foundation, insulation, or drainage review.
Neighborhood quality and school assignment vary by exact address.
Tornado and severe-weather risk should be included in insurance and maintenance planning.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale and transaction-cost risk are high.
Cheap properties with deferred maintenance can cost more over time than cleaner, slightly higher-priced homes.
buyer_notes: Little Rock AFB buyers should compare Jacksonville, Cabot, Sherwood, North Little Rock, Maumelle, Beebe, and Ward by commute, schools, condition, safety, taxes, and resale logic.
Do not buy only because the price is low; inspection quality and exit strategy matter heavily.
A clean, well-maintained home with strong HVAC, roof, drainage, and resale appeal may be better than the cheapest available option.
Cabot can appeal to families prioritizing schools and suburban lifestyle, but commute should be tested during real report-time windows.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who preserve monthly flexibility and avoid major deferred-maintenance exposure.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around Little Rock AFB commute, VA readiness, condition, roof/HVAC quality, storm-readiness, and practical monthly value.
Move-in-ready homes with clean inspection history and PCS-friendly timing can stand out.
Military buyers respond well to realistic payment logic and clear maintenance history.
Homes in strong Jacksonville, Cabot, or Sherwood commute corridors should emphasize base access, family practicality, and resale usability.
investor_angles: Military turnover, C-130 training demand, Central Arkansas workforce demand, and affordable acquisition costs support rental relevance.
Cash flow can work because acquisition prices are low compared with larger military metros.
Investors should stress-test vacancy, repairs, property taxes, HVAC replacement, roof life, tenant turnover, and neighborhood-specific rent demand.
Clean single-family homes in practical commute corridors may outperform cheaper properties with deferred maintenance or weak location.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in payment, not just list price, as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, utilities, commute, property condition, school assignment, and resale quality before locking a neighborhood.
This market rewards disciplined buyers more than emotional stretch buyers.
A lower purchase price can materially improve monthly flexibility, but only if the home has realistic condition, resale, and maintenance profile.
seller_guidance: Price against current inventory and days on market, not older peak assumptions.
Condition and presentation matter more when buyers have leverage.
Military-adjacent demand can be a strength if framed correctly with commute, condition, and monthly value.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near Little Rock AFB and Jacksonville commute corridors.
Investors should still model vacancy, tax drag, insurance, HVAC, roof life, and maintenance conservatively.
A good rental here is about stable spread, clean condition, and practical base access — not just headline affordability.

market.market_type_summary: Affordable Central Arkansas airlift market with low commute pressure, improving price trend, and property-condition-sensitive buyer leverage

base_profile.market_label: Jacksonville / Little Rock AFB Military Housing Market
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Little Rock AFB is a major Air Mobility Command C-130 installation near Jacksonville, Arkansas. The base supports tactical airlift, C-130 training, mobility operations, airlift readiness, and mission support functions tied closely to Central Arkansas.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Airlift-focused, affordable, commute-light, family-relevant, training-connected, condition-sensitive, and highly tied to Jacksonville, Cabot, Sherwood, North Little Rock, Beebe, and Central Arkansas housing choices.
base_profile.base_bluf: Little Rock AFB is not just a Jacksonville housing decision. It is a BAH, property-condition, school-zone, commute, storm-readiness, assignment-length, and resale-depth decision. The low prices can help, but the wrong home can still create maintenance and exit risk.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize assignment length, school-zone verification, BAH fit, roof/HVAC condition, commute route, property condition, and resale logic before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, roommate/rental flexibility, and access to food, gyms, Little Rock metro amenities, and daily services.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Little Rock AFB housing conversations are about BAH realism, commute simplicity, property condition, school-zone confidence, and Central Arkansas lifestyle fit — not generic Arkansas market stats.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Little Rock AFB is influenced by C-130 mission demand, training and permanent-party turnover, local workforce demand, and the limited depth of clean family-size rental inventory near Jacksonville and Cabot.

visitor_control_center.name: Little Rock AFB Visitor Center

visitor_control_center.hours: Monday-Friday, 0600-1630; closed holidays and Family Days; verify current hours before travel

visitor_control_center.note: Use official Little Rock AFB visitor and base access information before arrival. Access requirements, sponsor rules, gate procedures, and hours can change.

best_for: Users wanting services and short commute

watchouts: Neighborhood and property condition vary
Older homes may require stronger HVAC, roof, foundation, drainage, and plumbing inspection
School assignment should be verified by exact address

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Cabot

watchouts: Commute should be tested during real report-time windows

commute_intelligence.commute_bluf: For Little Rock AFB, commute risk is usually low if the user lives in Jacksonville, Cabot, or nearby Pulaski/Lonoke County corridors. Gate choice, school traffic, limited-hour gates, severe weather, and exact duty schedule still matter.
primary_commute_factors: School traffic and local road congestion
Exact duty location on or near the installation
Shift schedule or early report time

commute_bands.label: Close-in
commute_bands.fit: Best for users prioritizing proximity and daily convenience
commute_bands.watchout: Close-in areas still need property, school, and condition screening.

commute_bands.label: Balanced
commute_bands.fit: Often the best balance between housing quality, schools, services, and commute
commute_bands.watchout: Gate choice and exact home condition can still change the real answer.

commute_bands.label: Lifestyle tradeoff
commute_bands.fit: May work for users wanting stronger schools, more space, or broader metro access
commute_bands.watchout: Can become inconvenient for early reporting, frequent base errands, or family needs.

commute_bands.label: Extended commute
commute_bands.fit: Only for users with strong household reasons to live farther from base
commute_bands.watchout: Longer commute can overpower lower price or lifestyle appeal.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Use Vandenberg Gate as the primary 24/7 gate assumption.
Check Arnold Gate as an optional commute helper only when the user's route and schedule fit posted hours.
Do not rely on Harris Road Gate unless official sources confirm active hours.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on price.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, users should test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window they would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Little Rock AFB-area housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, property taxes, utilities, debt, condition, and assignment length. BAH may support buying comfortably in Jacksonville, Cabot, and nearby corridors, but short timelines and deferred-maintenance risk can make renting or on-base housing smarter for some users.
recommended_calculations: BAH with dependents vs estimated rent
BAH without dependents vs estimated rent
Total housing cost vs 30% income lane
Residual monthly income after expenses and housing
bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter, not a promise of affordability. The Base tab should push users into the PCSUnited financial calculator when the housing gap, assignment length, property condition, or maintenance exposure looks tight.

family_readiness.family_bluf: Families PCSing to Little Rock AFB should verify school zones, childcare options, medical access, spouse employment, commute, property condition, and housing availability before choosing a neighborhood.

school_guidance.school_zone_warning: Always verify school assignment by exact address before signing a lease or purchase contract.
school_guidance.nearby_school_district_note: Common Little Rock AFB-area school research may include Jacksonville North Pulaski School District, Cabot Public Schools, Pulaski County Special School District, North Little Rock School District, Beebe School District, and address-specific assignments across Pulaski and Lonoke County.
school_guidance.school_liaison_note: Use official school liaison resources where available for military school transition support.

childcare_guidance.cdc_note: Child Development Center availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified directly with official base resources.
childcare_guidance.youth_program_note: Youth programs and school-age care can materially affect family housing decisions.

medical_guidance.medical_warning: Verify clinic availability, pharmacy procedures, and emergency care options directly with official medical sources before relying on any single facility.

family_readiness.spouse_employment_note: Central Arkansas offers broader spouse employment than many small military towns, especially across healthcare, education, logistics, state government, retail, and Little Rock/North Little Rock employers, but commute and childcare should be considered together.

before_arrival: Verify official gate and Visitor Center hours.
Confirm sponsor contact and unit reporting instructions.
Confirm lodging or temporary housing plan.
Review housing office guidance before signing any lease or purchase contract.
Compare BAH against rent, mortgage, taxes, insurance, utilities, and debt.
Shortlist neighborhoods by likely gate and commute band.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Check pet policies for on-base housing, rentals, and temporary lodging.
first_week: Visit or contact the housing office if still deciding between on-base and off-base options.
Drive the commute from target neighborhoods during expected report time.
Tour properties before committing when possible.
Avoid choosing purely by low rent or low purchase price.
first_30_days: Finalize housing only after comparing commute, BAH fit, school zones, property condition, resale risk, weather exposure, and total monthly cost.
Save emergency, medical, gate, and housing office contact information.
Update PCSUnited profile and financial readiness dashboard with final housing numbers.
Review whether the selected area supports the user's assignment length and family needs.

pcs_watchouts.title: Gate hours can change
pcs_watchouts.message: Use official Little Rock AFB gate pages before travel or commute planning.

pcs_watchouts.title: Property condition matters heavily
pcs_watchouts.message: Low purchase prices can hide roof, HVAC, drainage, foundation, plumbing, electrical, or deferred-maintenance risk.

pcs_watchouts.title: Distance is not the full decision
pcs_watchouts.message: Neighborhoods should be compared by realistic gate access, school fit, property condition, storm exposure, and resale depth.

pcs_watchouts.title: School zones are address-specific
pcs_watchouts.message: Never assume a school assignment from neighborhood name alone.

pcs_watchouts.title: BAH is not the full affordability picture
pcs_watchouts.message: Users must compare rent or mortgage against income, debt, expenses, utilities, taxes, insurance, maintenance, and savings goals.

pcs_watchouts.title: Small-market resale risk is real
pcs_watchouts.message: A home that is easy to buy may not be equally easy to sell if condition, location, or timing is weak.

pcs_watchouts.title: Severe weather and drainage matter
pcs_watchouts.message: Tornado/severe-storm exposure, drainage, tree risk, and roof condition should be reviewed before calling a home affordable.

pcs_watchouts.title: On-base housing is not automatically the best financial answer
pcs_watchouts.message: Compare waitlist, BAH capture, commute, home condition, schools, assignment length, and lifestyle before deciding.

base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited is not an official DoD source. This Base tab is intended for public-facing PCS orientation, housing research, and relocation planning. Users should verify gate hours, access requirements, medical services, housing availability, school zones, market prices, insurance estimates, property condition, and official procedures directly with the installation or official service provider before travel, lease signing, or home purchase.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau / Jacksonville, Pulaski County, and Central Arkansas regional planning estimates

rent.provider: Zillow rental observations / local Jacksonville rental market estimates

home_price.provider: Zillow / Jacksonville and Little Rock AFB-area housing market observations

parser_notes: This Little Rock AFB file follows the same tempo and structure as the uploaded Lackland.json reference, including market metrics, costs, ownership notes, rental metrics, bedroom bands, housing market object, base_profile, gates, major services, on-base housing, recommended neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, family readiness, PCS watchouts, realtor intelligence, data confidence, source notes, and compatibility notes.
Base-facing label is set to Little Rock AFB, while Jacksonville and Central Arkansas are preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.
This file includes the shared placeholder base_image_url requested for bases without finished map images.
Gate and housing office details are aligned to official Little Rock AFB and Air Force Housing public sources as of June 27, 2026.
